feedback.text: I urge the EU to consider adding a firmer definition of digital ownership to the Digital Fairness Act that ensures consumers are guaranteed the continued use of their purchased digital goods (movies, books, music and videogames) even after the companies they have purchased them from stop the support or 'stocking' of said goods. Amazon, Spotify, Steam (or the publishers of the media in question) should not be able to remove content from user's libraries after purchase. This would protect media from becoming inaccessible or destroyed (see: the European Citizen's Initiative, 'stop destroying videogames'). I would also suggest broadening the scope of points 1. 'Dark patterns in online interfaces' and 2.'Addictive design' to be able to more directly regulate online media design that maximizes algorithm-based addictive short form content as these practices are predatory and harmful to the average user.
